heading: (intro)
raw: Directed by Frank Capra. Written by Sidney Buchman, based on the story "The Gentleman from Montana" by Lewis R. Foster.
clean: Directed by Frank Capra. Written by Sidney Buchman, based on the story "The Gentleman from Montana" by Lewis R. Foster.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: Did you ever have so much to say about something, you just couldn't say it?
clean: Did you ever have so much to say about something, you just couldn't say it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: Try sitting down.
clean: Try sitting down.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: I did - I got right back up again.
clean: I did - I got right back up again.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: Now look. Let's get down to particulars. How big is this thing? Where's it gonna be? How many boys will it accommodate? You've got to have all of that in it, you know.
clean: Now look. Let's get down to particulars. How big is this thing? Where's it gonna be? How many boys will it accommodate? You've got to have all of that in it, you know.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: Yeah, yeah, and something else, Miss Saunders. The uh, the spirit of it. The idea - the - [He snaps his fingers] How do ya say it? [He walks to the window in which the lighted Capitol Dome is seen. He points out at the Dome] That's what's got to be in it!
clean: Yeah, yeah, and something else, Miss Saunders. The uh, the spirit of it. The idea - the - How do ya say it? That's what's got to be in it!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: What?
clean: What?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: The Capitol Dome.
clean: The Capitol Dome.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: On paper? [She lifts her eyebrows a little]
clean: On paper?

heading: Dialogue
raw: James Taylor: What did you mean "count me out"?
clean: What did you mean "count me out"?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Paine: You can't pull that steamroller stuff. Your methods won't do here. However it happened, this boy's a Senator. This is Washington, Jim!
clean: You can't pull that steamroller stuff. Your methods won't do here. However it happened, this boy's a Senator. This is Washington, Jim!

heading: Dialogue
raw: James Taylor: My steamroller methods don't go here? They've done pretty well by you.
clean: My steamroller methods don't go here? They've done pretty well by you.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Paine: This boy is different. He's honest. He thinks the world of me. We can't do this to him!
clean: This boy is different. He's honest. He thinks the world of me. We can't do this to him!

heading: Dialogue
raw: James Taylor: Should I just stand around and let that drooling infant wrap that Willet Creek dam appropriation around my neck? Not me. Either he falls in line or I'll break him so open they won't find the pieces.
clean: Should I just stand around and let that drooling infant wrap that Willet Creek dam appropriation around my neck? Not me. Either he falls in line or I'll break him so open they won't find the pieces.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Paine: Jim, I won't stand for it.
clean: Jim, I won't stand for it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: James Taylor: You won't stand for it?
clean: You won't stand for it?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Paine: I don't want any part of crucifying this boy.
clean: I don't want any part of crucifying this boy.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Joseph Paine: Jim, you don't have to...
clean: Jim, you don't have to...

heading: Dialogue
raw: James Taylor: It's all right. Seems a shame to part company after all these years.
clean: It's all right. Seems a shame to part company after all these years.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: I see. When you get home, what are you gonna tell those kids?
clean: I see. When you get home, what are you gonna tell those kids?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: I'll tell 'em the truth. Might as well find it out now as later.
clean: I'll tell 'em the truth. Might as well find it out now as later.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: I don't think they'll believe you, Jeff. You know, they're liable to look up at you with hurt faces and say, 'Jeff, what did you do? Quit? Didn't you do something about it?'
clean: I don't think they'll believe you, Jeff. You know, they're liable to look up at you with hurt faces and say, 'Jeff, what did you do? Quit? Didn't you do something about it?'

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: Well, what do you expect me to do? An honorary stooge like me against the Taylors and Paines and machines and lies...
clean: Well, what do you expect me to do? An honorary stooge like me against the Taylors and Paines and machines and lies...

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: What? Do what, Saunders?
clean: What? Do what, Saunders?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: You just make up your mind you're not gonna quit and I'll tell you what. I've been thinkin' about it all the way back here. It's a forty foot dive into a tub of water, but I think you can do it.
clean: You just make up your mind you're not gonna quit and I'll tell you what. I've been thinkin' about it all the way back here. It's a forty foot dive into a tub of water, but I think you can do it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: Clarissa, where can we get a drink?
clean: Clarissa, where can we get a drink?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: [slapping his knee] Now you're talkin'!
clean: Now you're talkin'!

heading: Dialogue
raw: President of Senate: The Chair recognizes... Senator Smith!
clean: The Chair recognizes... Senator Smith!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: Thank you, sir.
clean: Thank you, sir.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: Diz, here we go.
clean: Diz, here we go.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Senator Joseph Paine: Mr. President, will the Senator yield?
clean: Mr. President, will the Senator yield?

heading: Dialogue
raw: President of Senate: Will the Senator yield?
clean: Will the Senator yield?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: No, sir, I'm afraid not, no sir. I yielded the floor once before, if you can remember, and I was practically never heard of again. No sir. And we might as well all get together on this yielding business right off the bat, now.
clean: No, sir, I'm afraid not, no sir. I yielded the floor once before, if you can remember, and I was practically never heard of again. No sir. And we might as well all get together on this yielding business right off the bat, now.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: Now, I had some pretty good coaching last night, and I find that if I yield only for a question or a point of order or a personal privilege, that I can hold this floor almost until doomsday. In other words, I've got a piece to speak, and blow hot or cold, I'm going to speak it.
clean: Now, I had some pretty good coaching last night, and I find that if I yield only for a question or a point of order or a personal privilege, that I can hold this floor almost until doomsday. In other words, I've got a piece to speak, and blow hot or cold, I'm going to speak it.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Senator Joseph Paine: I wish to ask my distinguished colleague, has he one scrap of evidence to add now to the defense he did not give and could not give at that same hearing?
clean: I wish to ask my distinguished colleague, has he one scrap of evidence to add now to the defense he did not give and could not give at that same hearing?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Jefferson Smith: I have no defense against forged papers!
clean: I have no defense against forged papers!

heading: Dialogue
raw: Senator Joseph Paine: The Committee ruled otherwise! The gentleman stands guilty, as charged. And I believe I speak for every member when I say that no one cares to hear what a man of his condemned character has to say about any section of any legislation before this House.
clean: The Committee ruled otherwise! The gentleman stands guilty, as charged. And I believe I speak for every member when I say that no one cares to hear what a man of his condemned character has to say about any section of any legislation before this House.

heading: Dialogue
raw: President of Senate: Order, order, gentlemen.
clean: Order, order, gentlemen.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Senator Joseph Paine: Will the Senator yield?
clean: Will the Senator yield?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Diz Moore: [dictating into phone] In protest, the whole Senate body rose and walked out.
clean: In protest, the whole Senate body rose and walked out.

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: No! No, not that straight stuff. Now listen, kick it up, get on his side, fight for him! Understand?
clean: No! No, not that straight stuff. Now listen, kick it up, get on his side, fight for him! Understand?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Diz Moore: You love this monkey - don't you?
clean: You love this monkey - don't you?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Clarissa Saunders: What do you think? Now listen, go to work. Do as I tell you, you do know that, don't you?
clean: What do you think? Now listen, go to work. Do as I tell you, you do know that, don't you?

heading: Dialogue
raw: Diz Moore: [into phone] Throw out that last, take this. This is the most titanic battle of modern times. A David without even a slingshot rises to do battle against the mighty Goliath, the Taylor machine, allegedly crooked inside and out. Yeah, and for my money and an extra bonus prize, you can cut out the "allegedly."
clean: Throw out that last, take this. This is the most titanic battle of modern times. A David without even a slingshot rises to do battle against the mighty Goliath, the Taylor machine, allegedly crooked inside and out. Yeah, and for my money and an extra bonus prize, you can cut out the "allegedly."
